item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t really like this, I find it to be exploitative in using violence against women without really exploring such a sticky subject in a satisfying way.

The writing is competent and the dialogue is good, but overall it left me feeling rather hollow for a piece about domestic violence

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for "[He lifts his shaking hands, as though in explanation.]"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed the slow-burn twist. Predictable, but that heightened the tension. The dialogue is good, utilitarian, gets the job done. The technical writing leaves a lot to be desired.

in an isolated forest of Tacoma, Washington

Why wouldn't the Foundation mention what forest?

The South-facing bedroom

Unnecessary capitalization.

enter the space, rather, it manifests

enter the space; rather, it manifests

I also have no idea why you made the primary anomaly the house and not the actual time travel room, considering the rest of the house is nonanomalous. I would've gone so far as to make the primary anomaly a temporal anomaly inside the room but even making it just the room would help. As it is, you've got a completely unnecessary subdesignation in the article.

Of course, these are all technical nitpicks, and the story is respectable enough to warrant an upvote. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the SCP was overall very well written, the structure of the writing was excellent and it's a concept I find very unique. On an additional note though, I noticed a *fine* mistake in the interview log, on the second to last line "Kelly" is referred to as "Myers", the agent doing the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. Simple, but the simplicity is played very well, and the entire story was both coherent and gripping. Thoroughly enjoyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dialogue is well written, and the story is tragic. I find the containment procedures to be a bit too simple, and the bulk of the article being that long last interview makes it drag a bit towards the end. I'm also not a fan of the melodramatic last line, but that's a personal preference. Overall I think theres enough here for a +1 for me.

item.title: Special Thanks
item.content:encoded: Thank you to Dyslexion and DrAkimoto for working with me on the concept, and thanks to Simartar, RockTeethMothEyes, and glewmie for giving me feedback on the drafts until it was nice and polished and ready for you all!
